sentence_reviews.text: The agent learns or uses a predictive environment model to evaluate and plan future actions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto, Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction, 2nd ed., model-based methods chapters/sections on models and planning; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control'.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support RL, models/planning, and control context; the exact wording is a concise synthesis rather than a directly quoted definition.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Model-based reinforcement learning is to connect Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Latent world models & predictive dynamics, World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book model-based/planning material; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning' and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control'; PML book1 long TOC latent-variable/modeling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reinforcement learning, planning/control, and latent-variable modeling are supported, but the complete graph bridge to the listed downstream capabilities is atlas synthesis and not directly established by the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Latent world models & predictive dynamics
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book model-based methods on environment models and planning; PML book1 long TOC latent-variable and dynamical/modeling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Predictive environment models support a connection to latent world models and dynamics, but the exact downstream atlas label is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sutton & Barto, model-based RL/planning sections covering simulated experience and planning from a model.
sentence_reviews.notes: Rollouts from learned or given models are consistent with model-based RL; 'imagination' is atlas terminology not directly established by the inspected reference pointer.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Game-playing, control & simulation agents
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning', Chapter 5 games, and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control'; RL book application/planning material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Games, RL, planning, and control are supported as related topics; simulation agents are not directly named in the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book reinforcement-learning setup/model-based context; PML latent-variable topics; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 27 AI limits/safety.
sentence_reviews.notes: The direct dependencies are plausible from cited topics, but the deployment-objective/data-generating-process limitation is generic atlas wording not directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Model-based reinforcement learning follows the learning setup expressed by Reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book model-based methods as a reinforcement-learning family; AIMA contents.html, Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning'.
sentence_reviews.notes: Model-based RL is a specialization of RL, so the trained_under/core relationship is defensible.

sentence_reviews.text: Model-based reinforcement learning uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book model/state representation and model-based planning material; PML book1 latent-variable and state-space modeling topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Hidden state is common in partially observed/world-model settings, but model-based RL can use fully observed state models; core strength is broader than the evidence supports.

sentence_reviews.text: Model-based reinforcement learning uses Multimodal, simulated & streaming data as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RL book model-based planning sections on simulated experience generated by models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Simulated experience is supported for model-based RL, but multimodal and streaming data are not directly supported by the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Model-based reinforcement learning uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control' and Chapter 22 'Reinforcement Learning'; RL book control/planning context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Control context is supported for RL/model-based planning; exact dynamical-systems wording is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Model-based RL is a subtype of reinforcement learning; trained_under/core is defensible.
Latent/hidden state is common for world models and partial observability, but not required for all model-based RL. Core strength is not fully supported.
Simulated rollouts are supported; multimodal and streaming data are not established as common from the inspected evidence.
Model-based RL commonly intersects planning/control over dynamical systems; uses/common is defensible.
